response:
The rental market in Miami, Florida, shows a wide range of prices. Single-family homes have the highest mean price. Condos and apartments show lower median values. Standard deviation indicates price variability. Outliers are identified using the IQR method. Property type significantly affects rental pricing.

* * *

Visualizing the data with Seaborn reveals distinct price distributions. Single-family homes cluster at higher price points. Condos show a more spread-out distribution. Apartments tend to have the lowest prices. Boxplots highlight outliers clearly. Different property types require separate analysis.

* * *

The analysis confirms Miami's rental market is highly segmented. Luxury condos have high standard deviations. Outliers may represent luxury or distressed properties. Policy makers should consider these trends. Investors may target specific property types. Further research could explore location impacts.
